content.text: I'll verify the pi-lsp extension implementation by reading SPEC.md and checking the created files and test results. Let me start.

# pi-lsp — Extension Specification

**Phiên bản:** 1.0.0-draft  
**Priority:** P1 — Sprint 2 (Tuần 5-7, song song với pi-pipeline)  
**Mục tiêu:** Mang code intelligence (LSP) vào Pi — go-to-definition, find-references, rename, diagnostics, code actions.  

---

## 1. Tổng Quan

oh-my-pi (can1357 fork) đã build LSP client hoàn chỉnh cho Pi. pi-lsp port implementation đó thành extension standalone.

### Nguồn cảm hứng — oh-my-pi LSP Implementation

**oh-my-pi đã chứng minh (`packages/coding-agent/src/lsp/`):**

| Feature | Implementation Detail |
|---------|----------------------|
| Client pooling | `Map<command:cwd, LSPClient>` — one server per language per workspace |
| syncContent() | Sync in-memory edits to LSP without disk read → instant diagnostics |
| lspmux multiplexing | Transparent wrapper for cross-window LSP sharing (rust-analyzer) |
| Full capabilities | hover, definition, typeDefinition, references, documentSymbol, rename, codeAction, formatting, rangeFormatting, publishDiagnostics |
| workspace/applyEdit | LSP servers can apply edits back |
| Idle timeout | Auto-shutdown after configurable idle period |
| TOML config | lspmux config via TOML, auto-detection with 5-min cache TTL |

---

## 2. Architecture

```
pi-lsp/
├── index.ts
├── src/
│   ├── client/
│   │   ├── manager.ts             # Client pool management
│   │   ├── client.ts              # Single LSP client (JSON-RPC 2.0 over stdio)
│   │   ├── connection.ts          # Transport layer (stdin/stdout)
│   │   ├── capabilities.ts        # Capability negotiation
│   │   └── lifecycle.ts           # Initialize, shutdown, exit
│   ├── features/
│   │   ├── hover.ts               # textDocument/hover
│   │   ├── definition.ts          # textDocument/definition + typeDefinition
│   │   ├── references.ts          # textDocument/references
│   │   ├── rename.ts              # textDocument/rename
│   │   ├── symbols.ts             # textDocument/documentSymbol + workspace/symbol
│   │   ├── diagnostics.ts         # textDocument/publishDiagnostics
│   │   ├── code-actions.ts        # textDocument/codeAction
│   │   ├── formatting.ts          # textDocument/formatting + rangeFormatting
│   │   └── edit.ts                # workspace/applyEdit handler
│   ├── sync/
│   │   ├── content-sync.ts        # syncContent() — in-memory → LSP
│   │   ├── file-watcher.ts        # File change detection
│   │   └── buffer-tracker.ts      # Track in-memory vs on-disk state
│   ├── servers/
│   │   ├── registry.ts            # Known LSP servers + detection
│   │   ├── typescript.ts          # tsserver configuration
│   │   ├── python.ts              # pylsp/pyright configuration
│   │   ├── rust.ts                # rust-analyzer configuration
│   │   ├── go.ts                  # gopls configuration
│   │   └── custom.ts              # User-configured LSP servers
│   ├── tools/
│   │   ├── lsp_hover.ts
│   │   ├── lsp_goto_def.ts
│   │   ├── lsp_find_refs.ts
│   │   ├── lsp_rename.ts
│   │   ├── lsp_diagnostics.ts
│   │   ├── lsp_symbols.ts
│   │   ├── lsp_code_actions.ts
│   │   └── lsp_format.ts
│   └── config.ts
├── package.json
└── tsconfig.json
```

---

## 3. Tools

### 3.1 `lsp_hover(file, line, column)` → Type Info & Docs
```typescript
{ file: string, line: number, column: number }
// Returns: type signature, documentation, source location
```

### 3.2 `lsp_goto_def(file, line, column)` → Jump to Definition
```typescript
{ file: string, line: number, column: number }
// Returns: { file, line, column } of definition
```

### 3.3 `lsp_find_refs(file, line, column)` → All References
```typescript
{ file: string, line: number, column: number, includeDeclaration?: boolean }
// Returns: Array<{ file, line, column, text }>
```

### 3.4 `lsp_rename(file, line, column, newName)` → Rename Symbol
```typescript
{ file: string, line: number, column: number, newName: string }
// Returns: { changes: Map<file, Edit[]> } preview, requires confirmation
```

### 3.5 `lsp_diagnostics(file?)` → Errors & Warnings
```typescript
{ file?: string }  // If omitted, return all diagnostics
// Returns: Array<{ file, line, column, severity, message, source }>
```

### 3.6 `lsp_symbols(query?)` → Workspace Symbol Search
```typescript
{ query?: string, file?: string }
// If query: workspace symbol search. If file: document symbols.
// Returns: Array<{ name, kind, file, line, containerName? }>
```

### 3.7 `lsp_code_actions(file, line, column)` → Quick Fixes
```typescript
{ file: string, line: number, column: number, diagnostics?: Diagnostic[] }
// Returns: Array<{ title, kind, edit? }>
```

### 3.8 `lsp_format(file, startLine?, endLine?)` → Format Code
```typescript
{ file: string, startLine?: number, endLine?: number }
// Returns: formatted text (or edits)
```

---

## 4. Hooks

### 4.1 Hook: `tool_result` (edit/write) → Auto-Diagnostics

```
tool_result event (event.toolName === "edit" || event.toolName === "write")
  → Extract text content from event.content (TextContent[])
  → syncContent() to LSP with updated file content
  → Get diagnostics for modified file
  → If errors found:
    → Return ToolResultEventResult with appended diagnostic info:
      { content: [...event.content, { type: "text", text: "\n⚠️ LSP issues in <file>:\n  Line 42: Type error..." }] }
```

### 4.2 Hook: `session_start` → Start LSP Servers

```
session_start event
  → Detect project languages (package.json → TS, requirements.txt → Python, Cargo.toml → Rust, go.mod → Go)
  → Pre-warm LSP servers for detected languages
  → Register file watchers
```

### 4.3 Hook: `session_shutdown` → Shutdown Servers

```
session_shutdown event
  → Send shutdown + exit to all active LSP servers
  → Clean up temp files
```

---

## 5. LSP Server Registry

```typescript
interface LSPServerConfig {
  language: string;
  command: string[];          // e.g., ["npx", "typescript-language-server", "--stdio"]
  extensions: string[];       // [".ts", ".tsx", ".js", ".jsx"]
  detectionFiles: string[];   // ["tsconfig.json", "package.json"]
  installHint?: string;       // "npm install -g typescript-language-server typescript"
}

const DEFAULT_SERVERS: LSPServerConfig[] = [
  {
    language: "typescript",
    command: ["npx", "typescript-language-server", "--stdio"],
    extensions: [".ts", ".tsx", ".js", ".jsx"],
    detectionFiles: ["tsconfig.json", "package.json"],
    installHint: "npm install -g typescript-language-server typescript"
  },
  {
    language: "python",
    command: ["pylsp", "--stdio"],  // or pyright-langserver --stdio
    extensions: [".py"],
    detectionFiles: ["requirements.txt", "pyproject.toml", "setup.py"],
    installHint: "pip install python-lsp-server"
  },
  {
    language: "rust",
    command: ["rust-analyzer"],  // supports lspmux for multiplexing
    extensions: [".rs"],
    detectionFiles: ["Cargo.toml"],
    installHint: "rustup component add rust-analyzer"
  },
  {
    language: "go",
    command: ["gopls", "serve"],
    extensions: [".go"],
    detectionFiles: ["go.mod"],
    installHint: "go install golang.org/x/tools/gopls@latest"
  }
];
```

User can add custom servers via config.

---

## 6. syncContent() — Key Innovation

**oh-my-pi's key pattern:**

When agent edits a file in memory (before saving to disk):
1. Send `textDocument/didChange` to LSP with in-memory content
2. LSP updates its internal model WITHOUT reading from disk
3. Diagnostics reflect the UNSAVED state
4. Agent gets instant feedback: "this edit introduces a type error"

Without syncContent: agent must save file → LSP detects file change → diagnostics update (slow, requires round-trip through file system).

---

## 7. Configuration

`.pi/pi-lsp.json`:
```json
{
  "enabled": true,
  "autoStart": true,
  "idleTimeoutMs": 300000,
  "syncContent": true,
  "autoDiagnostics": true,
  "servers": {
    "typescript": { "enabled": true },
    "python": { "enabled": true, "command": ["pyright-langserver", "--stdio"] },
    "rust": { "enabled": true, "lspmux": true },
    "go": { "enabled": true }
  },
  "customServers": []
}
```

---

## 8. Integration Points

### pi-lsp → pi-pipeline
- Verification gates use `lsp_diagnostics` to check for type errors after edits
- Code

{"type":"message_end","message":{"role":"toolResult","content":[{"type":"text","text":"# pi-lsp — Extension Specification

**Phiên bản:** 1.0.0-draft  
**Priority:** P1 — Sprint 2 (Tuần 5-7, song song với pi-pipeline)  
**Mục tiêu:** Mang code intelligence (LSP) vào Pi — go-to-definition, find-references, rename, diagnostics, code actions.  

---

## 1. Tổng Quan

oh-my-pi (can1357 fork) đã build LSP client hoàn chỉnh cho Pi. pi-lsp port implementation đó thành extension standalone.

### Nguồn cảm hứng — oh-my-pi LSP Implementation

**oh-my-pi đã chứng minh (`packages/coding-agent/src/lsp/`):**

| Feature | Implementation Detail |
|---------|----------------------|
| Client pooling | `Map<command:cwd, LSPClient>` — one server per language per workspace |
| syncContent() | Sync in-memory edits to LSP without disk read → instant diagnostics |
| lspmux multiplexing | Transparent wrapper for cross-window LSP sharing (rust-analyzer) |
| Full capabilities | hover, definition, typeDefinition, references, documentSymbol, rename, codeAction, formatting, rangeFormatting, publishDiagnostics |
| workspace/applyEdit | LSP servers can apply edits back |
| Idle timeout | Auto-shutdown after configurable idle period |
| TOML config | lspmux config via TOML, auto-detection with 5-min cache TTL |

---

## 2. Architecture

```
pi-lsp/
├── index.ts
├── src/
│   ├── client/
│   │   ├── manager.ts             # Client pool management
│   │   ├── client.ts              # Single LSP client (JSON-RPC 2.0 over stdio)
│   │   ├── connection.ts          # Transport layer (stdin/stdout)
│   │   ├── capabilities.ts        # Capability negotiation
│   │   └── lifecycle.ts           # Initialize, shutdown, exit
│   ├── features/
│   │   ├── hover.ts               # textDocument/hover
│   │   ├── definition.ts          # textDocument/definition + typeDefinition
│   │   ├── references.ts          # textDocument/references
│   │   ├── rename.ts              # textDocument/rename
│   │   ├── symbols.ts             # textDocument/documentSymbol + workspace/symbol
│   │   ├── diagnostics.ts         # textDocument/publishDiagnostics
│   │   ├── code-actions.ts        # textDocument/codeAction
│   │   ├── formatting.ts          # textDocument/formatting + rangeFormatting
│   │   └── edit.ts                # workspace/applyEdit handler
│   ├── sync/
│   │   ├── content-sync.ts        # syncContent() — in-memory → LSP
│   │   ├── file-watcher.ts        # File change detection
│   │   └── buffer-tracker.ts      # Track in-memory vs on-disk state
│   ├── servers/
│   │   ├── registry.ts            # Known LSP servers + detection
│   │   ├── typescript.ts          # tsserver configuration
│   │   ├── python.ts              # pylsp/pyright configuration
│   │   ├── rust.ts                # rust-analyzer configuration
│   │   ├── go.ts                  # gopls configuration
│   │   └── custom.ts              # User-configured LSP servers
│   ├── tools/
│   │   ├── lsp_hover.ts
│   │   ├── lsp_goto_def.ts
│   │   ├── lsp_find_refs.ts
│   │   ├── lsp_rename.ts
│   │   ├── lsp_diagnostics.ts
│   │   ├── lsp_symbols.ts
│   │   ├── lsp_code_actions.ts
│   │   └── lsp_format.ts
│   └── config.ts
├── package.json
└── tsconfig.json
```

---

## 3. Tools

### 3.1 `lsp_hover(file, line, column)` → Type Info & Docs
```typescript
{ file: string, line: number, column: number }
// Returns: type signature, documentation, source location
```

### 3.2 `lsp_goto_def(file, line, column)` → Jump to Definition
```typescript
{ file: string, line: number, column: number }
// Returns: { file, line, column } of definition
```

### 3.3 `lsp_find_refs(file, line, column)` → All References
```typescript
{ file: string, line: number, column: number, includeDeclaration?: boolean }
// Returns: Array<{ file, line, column, text }>
```

### 3.4 `lsp_rename(file, line, column, newName)` → Rename Symbol
```typescript
{ file: string, line: number, column: number, newName: string }
// Returns: { changes: Map<file, Edit[]> } preview, requires confirmation
```

### 3.5 `lsp_diagnostics(file?)` → Errors & Warnings
```typescript
{ file?: string }  // If omitted, return all diagnostics
// Returns: Array<{ file, line, column, severity, message, source }>
```

### 3.6 `lsp_symbols(query?)` → Workspace Symbol Search
```typescript
{ query?: string, file?: string }
// If query: workspace symbol search. If file: document symbols.
// Returns: Array<{ name, kind, file, line, containerName? }>
```

### 3.7 `lsp_code_actions(file, line, column)` → Quick Fixes
```typescript
{ file: string, line: number, column: number, diagnostics?: Diagnostic[] }
// Returns: Array<{ title, kind, edit? }>
```

### 3.8 `lsp_format(file, startLine?, endLine?)` → Format Code
```typescript
{ file: string, startLine?: number, endLine?: number }
// Returns: formatted text (or edits)
```

---

## 4. Hooks

### 4.1 Hook: `tool_result` (edit/write) → Auto-Diagnostics

```
tool_result event (event.toolName === "edit" || event.toolName === "write")
  → Extract text content from event.content (TextContent[])
  → syncContent() to LSP with updated file content
  → Get diagnostics for modified file
  → If errors found:
    → Return ToolResultEventResult with appended diagnostic info:
      { content: [...event.content, { type: "text", text: "\n⚠️ LSP issues in <file>:\n  Line 42: Type error..." }] }
```

### 4.2 Hook: `session_start` → Start LSP Servers

```
session_start event
  → Detect project languages (package.json → TS, requirements.txt → Python, Cargo.toml → Rust, go.mod → Go)
  → Pre-warm LSP servers for detected languages
  → Register file watchers
```

### 4.3 Hook: `session_shutdown` → Shutdown Servers

```
session_shutdown event
  → Send shutdown + exit to all active LSP servers
  → Clean up temp files
```

---

## 5. LSP Server Registry

```typescript

  detectionFiles: string[];   // ["tsconfig.json", "package.json"]
  installHint?: string;       // "npm install -g typescript-language-server typescript"
}

const DEFAULT_SERVERS: LSPServerConfig[] = [
  {
    language: "typescript",

    installHint: "npm install -g typescript-language-server typescript"
  },
  {
    language: "python",
    command: ["pylsp", "--stdio"],  // or pyright-langserver --stdio
    extensions: [".py"],
    detectionFiles: ["requirements.txt", "pyproject.toml", "setup.py"],
    installHint: "pip install python-lsp-server"
  },
  {
    language: "rust",
    command: ["rust-analyz
